chunks.text: On December 17, 1903, on a sand dune at Kitty Hawk, North Carolina
the Wright brothers succeeded in a twelve second flight.
They were brothers who, while running a bicycle shop
had kept holding a dream of flying.
Wilbur and Orville, with their own hands
built a wind tunnel and examined the shape of wings over and over again.
Many people said it was impossible.
But the brothers never denied their dream.
" If birds can fly, we surely can too," they believed.
The wind was strong that morning.
The aircraft, carrying Orville, performed its brief flight of twelve seconds.
The distance was only forty meters.
Yet that moment was the triumph of a long battle against gravity
which humanity itself had finally overcome.
The moment his brother landed, Wilbur quietly took his hand.
To be called an inventor meant nothing to them at that instant.
It was the very first step that humanity had taken toward the sky.
